tests.name: init_sh_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh hung in an Invalid choice loop after the project skeleton + tool plan + repo creation prompts; was killed manually. Project skeleton and phase-state.json were written before the hang, so subsequent tests proceeded. Heredoc driver fragility per runbook expectation.

tests.name: context7_detection
tests.details: verify-install.sh reports [OK] Context7 MCP installed and configured

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode for UAT; heredoc driver fragile
framework_bugs.reproduction: Pipe canned answers via heredoc to bash init.sh — at some point past the post-create prompts the script enters a 'Invalid choice. Enter a number between 1 and 2.' loop that never receives further input and never exits.
framework_bugs.expected: init.sh should accept --non-interactive flag with config (project name/platform/track/deployment/governance/language/dir/host/visibility) so UAT can drive deterministically; or at minimum prompt count and option counts should match the documented sequence.
framework_bugs.actual: init.sh hung indefinitely on a 2-option prompt; only --dry-run and --help flags exist. Project skeleton was created before the hang, but completion never reported and no exit code captured.

documentation_gaps.gap: init.sh prompt sequence not documented for UAT/automation; heredoc consumers must read source to derive answer order, including conditional branches (governance, full-track-personal confirm, host-aware repo creation visibility/URL/attestation).

init.sh does not write .claude/intake-progress.json, but downstream scripts (upgrade-project.sh) require it as the canonical poc_mode source; the only writer is intake-wizard.sh. No documented requirement that wizard be run after init before upgrade.

tests.name: baseline_built
tests.details: Sponsored POC baseline = phase-state.json poc_mode=sponsored_poc, deployment=organizational, track=full. Skipped feature recording for upgrade test (runbook permits Phase 2 minimum but does not strictly require a recorded feature for upgrade-project.sh --to-production execution).

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: After workaround (writing .claude/intake-progress.json with poc_mode=sponsored_poc), 'bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production </dev/null' exited 0. First attempt without workaround failed with 'Project is not in POC mode' because upgrade-project.sh does not consult .claude/phase-state.json for poc_mode.

tests.name: phase_state_preserved
tests.details: current_phase=0 unchanged; project name unchanged across upgrade; track=full preserved; deployment=organizational preserved

tests.name: process_state_preserved
tests.details: .claude/process-state.json (build_loop, uat_session, phase3/4, phase2_init) byte-identical pre/post upgrade

tests.name: new_required_artifacts_listed
tests.details: Upgrade output listed POC mode removal, phase-state update, intake-progress.json update, and ran verify-install.sh which warned about ~276 blank PROJECT_INTAKE.md fields and listed required pre-Phase-0 work

tests.name: pending_approval_sentinel_respected
tests.details: upgrade-project.sh does not check .claude/pending-approval.json. Source has no reference to the sentinel except a one-line comment in the changelog header.

tests.name: upgrade_changelog_present_in_script
tests.details: upgrade-project.sh header lines 11-21 reference both BL-006 (2026-04-24) and BL-015 (2026-04-25); 2 grep matches confirmed

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh requires intake-progress.json that init.sh never creates
framework_bugs.reproduction: 1) bash init.sh and let it create a sponsored_poc project (phase-state.json has poc_mode='sponsored_poc'); 2) without running intake-wizard.sh, run 'bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production </dev/null'. Result: '[FAIL] Project is not in POC mode. Use --track and/or --deployment for non-POC upgrades.' Exit 1.
framework_bugs.expected: upgrade-project.sh should fall back to .claude/phase-state.json's poc_mode field when intake-progress.json is missing or has empty/null poc_mode (mirroring how it falls back for CURRENT_TRACK).
framework_bugs.actual: Lines 188-205 only read poc_mode from intake-progress.json; no fallback to phase-state.json. Workaround for this UAT was to hand-write .claude/intake-progress.json, after which the upgrade succeeded cleanly.

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh does not honor BL-015 pending-approval sentinel
framework_bugs.reproduction: Touch .claude/pending-approval.json with a valid schema in a sponsored_poc project, then run 'bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production'. Script proceeds (or fails for unrelated reasons) without acknowledging the sentinel.
framework_bugs.expected: Per the runbook's pending_approval_sentinel_respected assertion and the spirit of BL-015, structural changes (deployment/track/POC removal) should refuse-or-warn while a pending-approval sentinel is active.
framework_bugs.actual: No sentinel check anywhere in scripts/upgrade-project.sh; only a one-line comment in the script header references BL-015.

documentation_gaps.gap: Builder's Guide / upgrade docs do not mention that intake-progress.json is required for upgrade-project.sh to detect POC mode, nor that intake-wizard.sh must be run between init.sh and any upgrade.

BL-015 sentinel scope is unclear: only pre-commit-gate enforces it. If the intent is broader 'no structural mutations during pending approval', upgrade-project.sh, intake-wizard.sh, and process-checklist.sh should all consult it.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: 5 grep matches in project files but all are legitimate: web.md mentions 'token strategy (JWT vs sessions)' and 'hardcoded passwords' (Bandit description); .github/workflows/release.yml has commented-out '${{ secrets.VERCEL_TOKEN }}' template comments; .github/workflows/ci.yml uses 'GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}' which is the standard Actions placeholder, not a committed secret.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — .claude/process-audit.log absent and no FORCE/reset events occurred during this UAT run

summary: Upgrade from sponsored_poc to production succeeded only after a manual workaround; init.sh hangs late in interactive flow and upgrade-project.sh fails to detect poc_mode without intake-wizard's intake-progress.json.
